item.title: Fiji Men and Fijiana 7s finish sixth at Bordeaux 7s
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026 The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team finished their Bordeaux 7s campaign in sixth place after a 17-14 defeat to the United States in the fifth-place playoff.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026

The Fiji Airways Men's National 7s team finished their Bordeaux 7s campaign in sixth place after a 17-14 defeat to the United States in the fifth-place playoff.

Fiji raced to a 14-0 lead in the first half, with Vuiviawa Naduvalo scoring a double, but were unable to maintain their advantage after the break.

The Americans responded strongly in the second half, crossing through David Still, Will Chevalier, and Lucas Lacamp to secure the win.

Fiji ended the World Rugby Sevens Championship in sixth place on 36 points.

The Fijiana 7s side also finished sixth at the Bordeaux 7s after falling 24-12 to Canada in their fifth-place playoff match.

They finished in seventh place with 26 points on the Championship standings.

Both teams have also qualified for the 2026/2027 World Rugby 7s Series.

Meanwhile, host France defeated New Zealand 14-5 to claim the men's title, and Australia beat New Zealand 26-19 to win the women's division.

item.title: Naduvalo and Veilawa named in Men's SVNS Dream Team of the Year
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026 After impressive performances throughout the 2025/2026 World Rugby SVNS Series, Fiji Airways Men's 7s representatives Vuiviawa Naduvalo and Terio Veilawa have been named in the Men's SVNS Dream Team of the Year.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026

After impressive performances throughout the 2025/2026 World Rugby SVNS Series, Fiji Airways Men's 7s representatives Vuiviawa Naduvalo and Terio Veilawa have been named in the Men's SVNS Dream Team of the Year.

Also included in the Dream Team are South Africa's Tristan Leyds and Shilton van Wyk, Australia's Harry Hutchison, Spain's Jeremy Trevithick, and Argentina's Luciano González.

Leyds was also named Men's SVNS Player of the Year, while New Zealand's Kele Lasaqa received the Men's SVNS Rookie of the Year award.

item.title: Panthers crush Wests Tigers 68-0
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026 The Penrith Panthers continued their dominance in the NRL with a 68-0 thrashing of the Wests Tigers.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/06/2026

The Penrith Panthers continued their dominance in the NRL with a 68-0 thrashing of the Wests Tigers.

The Panthers ran in 12 unanswered tries, with talisman Nathan Cleary contributing 24 points through a try and nine conversions.

Brian To'o, Freddy Lussick, and Thomas Jenkins each scored doubles, while Paul Alamoti, Isaah Yeo, Jack Cogger, Izack Tago, and Lindsay Smith also crossed for tries.

The Panther lead the points table with 26 points.

item.title: Fiji eyes strong finish against USA in Bordeaux
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 07/06/2026 The Fiji Airways Men’s National 7s team will need to improve their discipline and defence if they hope to finish strong at the final tournament of the World Rugby Sevens Championship in France.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 07/06/2026

The Fiji Airways Men’s National 7s team will need to improve their discipline and defence if they hope to finish strong at the final tournament of the World Rugby Sevens Championship in France.

Fiji topped its pool after going undefeated in the group stages, but ill-discipline proved costly in their quarter-final defeat to South Africa.

Fiji led 12-0 before a yellow card to Vuiviwa Naduvalo shifted the momentum in favour of the Blitzboks. South Africa capitalised on the numerical advantage, scoring a try to reduce the deficit to 12-7 at halftime.

Fiji will now face the United States in the fifth-place playoff.

The USA topped their pool after defeating Australia, Spain and Uruguay, and will be highly motivated as the result could determine their qualification for the new World Sevens Series competition, which begins later this year.

Fiji currently sits fourth on the standings with 26 points, while the USA is second from bottom with five points.

The two sides will meet at 1.06am tomorrow.

Meanwhile, France will take on South Africa in the first Cup semi-final at 11.14pm tonight, while New Zealand will face Spain in the second semi-final at 11.36pm.

The men's Cup final is scheduled for 3.41am tomorrow.

item.title: Sickening Wallace Sititi blow sours Chiefs’ Super Rugby Pacific qualifying final win over Reds
item.description: The Chiefs have set up a blockbuster home Super Rugby Pacific semifinal against the Crusaders, but star No 8 Wallace Sititi will b

item.title: England build towards World Cup opener with win over New Zealand
item.description: England coach Thomas Tuchel is expected to name a stronger lineup in the team's final World Cup warm-up match against Costa Rica after a 1-0 win over New Zealand. Harry Kane scored the winner as England continued their preparations in Tampa, with Tuchel using 22 players across the match to assess his squad options.
item.content:encoded:
England coach Thomas Tuchel is expected to name a stronger lineup in the team's final World Cup warm-up match against Costa Rica after a 1-0 win over New Zealand.

Harry Kane scored the winner as England continued their preparations in Tampa, with Tuchel using 22 players across the match to assess his squad options.

With England's opening World Cup clash against Croatia approaching, Tuchel is now expected to move away from experimentation and focus on building momentum with his preferred starting side.

The German coach was pleased with England's second-half performance and confirmed there were no injury concerns following the match.

item.title: Scotland gain confidence with convincing win ahead of World Cup
item.description: Scotland boosted their confidence ahead of the FIFA World Cup with a convincing win over Bolivia in challenging conditions in New Jersey. Despite temperatures topping 32 degrees Celsius, the Scots remained patient, precise and clinical, dominating the match and scoring four goals.
item.content:encoded:
Scotland boosted their confidence ahead of the FIFA World Cup with a convincing win over Bolivia in challenging conditions in New Jersey.

Despite temperatures topping 32 degrees Celsius, the Scots remained patient, precise and clinical, dominating the match and scoring four goals.

Bolivia, who are used to playing in difficult conditions and at high altitudes, struggled to cope with Scotland's intensity and attacking pressure.

While Bolivia are not among the world's strongest teams, the result gives Scotland valuable momentum ahead of their World Cup opener next Saturday.

item.title: Southern Zone: Grammar beat Marist 24-12 in U18 opener
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 07/06/2026 The Suva Grammar School U18 rugby team began their Vodafone Southern Zone Secondary School Rugby campaign on a high note, defeating rivals, Marist Brothers High School 24-12.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 07/06/2026

The Suva Grammar School U18 rugby team began their Vodafone Southern Zone Secondary School Rugby campaign on a high note, defeating rivals, Marist Brothers High School 24-12.

The match was played at the Suva Grammar Ground.

Meanwhile, in other U18 results, defending champions, Nasinu Secondary School thrashed Church College Fiji 48-3.

In the Eastern Zone, RKS thrashed Sila Central High School 46-3, and Lelean Memorial School defeated Queen Victoria School 26-10.

item.description: Tete Yengi scored on his debut as Australia held Switzerland to a 1-all draw in their final warm-up match before the FIFA World Cu

item.title: Canada defeat Fijiana to book semi final spot
item.description: The Fiji Airways Fijiana National 7s team suffered a quarter-final defeat to Canada 29-14 at the Bordeaux 7s today.Canada opened t

item.title: Iran accuses US of denying visas to football staff ahead of World Cup
item.description: Iran has accused the United States of denying visas to key members of its national football team's support staff ahead of the FIFA

item.title: Chiefs through to semifinals after 46-24 win over the Reds
item.description: The Chiefs have booked their Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific semi-final spot after defeating the Reds 46-24 last night.Damien McKe
